perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee, and Licensee hereby accepts from Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark solely and exclusively as a component of Licensee's own corporate name and in connection with marketing the investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services that Investment Advisor may provide to Licensee.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee, and Licensee hereby accepts from Licensor, a non-transferable, non-exclusive, royalty-bearing right and license to use the Licensed Mark solely and exclusively as a component of Licensee's own corporate name and in connection with supporting documentation provided by the Investment Advisor to Licensee.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'royalty-free' to 'royalty-bearing' and investment advisory services to supporting documentation introduces a contradiction. Previously, the Licensee was granted a right to use the Licensed Mark without needing to pay any royalties. The change now introduces royalties, which are not defined anywhere in the contract causing an in-text contradiction. The change from investment advisory services to supporting documentation introduces another contradiction as the advisory services were clearly defined while supporting documentation is not, leading to uncertainties about the scope of authorized use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee, and Licensee hereby accepts from Licensor, a non-transferable, non-exclusive, royalty-bearing right and license to use the Licensed Mark solely and exclusively as a component of Licensee's own corporate name and in connection with supporting documentation provided by the Investment Advisor to Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this License, and except as provided above, neither Licensee nor any affiliate, owner, director, officer, employee or agent thereof shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivative thereof in the Territory without the prior express written consent of Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion and shall not use the Licensed Mark for any purpose outside the Territory. All rights not expressly granted to Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'royalty-free' to 'royalty-bearing' in the changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text specifies no royalties, while the changed text introduces royalties, which are also mentioned in the contradicted text, but the contract does not define or explain how royalties will be assessed. This makes the contract ambiguous. Additionally, the change from 'investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services' to 'supporting documentation provided by the Investment Advisor' creates another contradiction because 'supporting documentation' is not clearly defined, leading to uncertainties about the scope of authorized use, whereas, 'investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services' is a defined scope.

perturbation.original_text: In order to preserve the inherent value of the Licensed Mark, Licensee agrees to use reasonable efforts to ensure that it maintains the quality of the Licensee's business and the operation thereof equal to the standards prevailing in the operation of Licensee's business as of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: In order to preserve the inherent value of the Licensed Mark, Licensee agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that it maintains the quality of the Licensee's business and the operation thereof equal to the standards prevailing in the operation of other firms within the same industry, even if exceeding standards of the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'reasonable efforts' to 'commercially reasonable efforts' and from 'Licensee's business as of the Effective Date' to 'other firms within the same industry, even if exceeding standards of the Effective Date' introduces a contradiction. 'Reasonable efforts' implies an obligation to maintain existing standards of the licensee, while 'commercially reasonable efforts' related to other firms may require the Licensee to expend more resources than initially anticipated, creating a disagreement about what actions need to be taken.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Quality Control. In order to preserve the inherent value of the Licensed Mark, Licensee agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that it maintains the quality of the Licensee's business and the operation thereof equal to the standards prevailing in the operation of other firms within the same industry, even if exceeding standards of the Effective Date. The Licensee further agrees to use the Licensed Mark in accordance with such quality standards as may be reasonably established by Licensor and communicated to the Licensee from time to time in writing, or as may be agreed to by Licensor and the Licensee from time to time in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text does not create a strong contradiction with the contradicted text. While the change alters the standard of effort and comparison, both sections ultimately aim to preserve the value of the Licensed Mark by focusing on quality standards. The contradicted text repeats the new standard as well, as the modification was a replacement, removing the old language. Therefore, a reader wouldn't immediately recognize incompatibility.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall immediately notify the other party and provide to the other party all relevant background facts upon becoming aware of (a) any registrations of, or applications for registration of, marks in the Territory that do or may conflict with the Licensor's rights in the Licensed Mark or the rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement, (b) any infringements or misuse of the Licensed Mark in the Territory by any third party ("Third Party Infringement"), or (c) any claim that Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark infringes the intellectual property rights of any third party in the Territory ("Third Party Claim").
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall notify the other party within 10 business days and provide to the other party a summary of the relevant facts upon becoming aware of (a) any registrations of, or applications for registration of, marks in the Territory that do or may conflict with the Licensor's rights in the Licensed Mark or the rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement, (b) any infringements or misuse of the Licensed Mark in the Territory by any third party ("Third Party Infringement"), or (c) any claim that Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark infringes the intellectual property rights of any third party in the Territory ("Third Party Claim").
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'immediately notify' to 'notify within 10 business days' and 'all relevant background facts' to 'a summary of the relevant facts' introduces a contradiction. The term immediately implies instantaneous notification. However, the additional text changes it to 10 business days which introduces a contradiction of an immediate requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notification of Infringement. Each party shall notify the other party within 10 business days and provide to the other party a summary of the relevant facts upon becoming aware of (a) any registrations of, or applications for registration of, marks in the Territory that do or may conflict with the Licensor's rights in the Licensed Mark or the rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement, (b) any infringements or misuse of the Licensed Mark in the Territory by any third party ("Third Party Infringement"), or (c) any claim that Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark infringes the intellectual property rights of any third party in the Territory ("Third Party Claim"). Licensor shall have the exclusive right, but not the obligation, to prosecute, defend and/or settle in its sole discretion, all actions, proceedings and claims involving any Third Party Infringement or Third Party Claim, and to take any other action that it deems necessary or proper for the protection and preservation of its rights in the Licensed Mark. Licensee shall cooperate with Licensor in the prosecution, defense or settlement of such actions, proceedings or claims.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'immediately notify' with 'notify within 10 business days' and 'all relevant background facts' with 'a summary of the relevant facts.' Although this represents a change in the required action, the Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed text. Therefore, there is no contradiction.
